plain_english: Wins with timing and anticipation from structure
mechanism: Reads leverage early and releases the ball before windows fully declare
nfl_consequence: Lets the passing game stay on schedule without forcing late-platform hero ball
use_when: Use for quarterbacks who consistently throw on time before windows fully open
avoid_when: Do not use for toolsy creators who rely mostly on late reaction playmaking
sample_phrase: Works from the pocket with anticipation and rhythm to beat leverage before the window closes.

plain_english: Can win from rhythm and create outside structure when needed
mechanism: Stays organized in rhythm and can reset the rep after pocket movement without losing downfield intent
nfl_consequence: Preserves play viability when structure moves off the spot
use_when: Use when the player shows both timing value and controlled off-script playmaking
avoid_when: Do not use when off-script is the only real source of value
sample_phrase: Can stay on schedule from structure and still salvage plays when the pocket moves off the spot.
notes: Good bridge phrase for modern starters

plain_english: Wins with sudden pacing and separation skill more than straight-line build speed
mechanism: Manipulates leverage with stem tempo body control and instant redirection instead of pure long speed
nfl_consequence: Creates target windows late in the route even when he is not simply running by defenders
use_when: Use for receivers who create space with pacing body control and instant change of direction rather than pure long speed
avoid_when: Do not use for true vertical burners whose game is driven by top gear
sample_phrase: Creates separation with sudden pacing and body control; he plays quicker than he times and consistently stresses leverage at the top of routes.

plain_english: A back whose value survives early downs third down and pass protection
mechanism: Presses tracks cleanly creates through contact and keeps protection rules stable enough to stay on the field
nfl_consequence: Keeps the offense in its full menu because the role does not have to change on passing downs
use_when: Use when the player shows rushing efficiency receiving utility and real pass-protection value
avoid_when: Do not use for explosive runners who have not shown third-down trust
sample_phrase: Projects as a complete back whose value holds on all three downs because the pass-protection floor is good enough to keep him on the field.

plain_english: Prototype misses exist but the player wins anyway because the functional traits are strong enough
mechanism: Length or frame thresholds are lighter than ideal so the rush has to stay early and forceful
trigger: When longer tackles keep the chest clean or widen the track before contact
nfl_consequence: The margin for error shrinks unless burst leverage and power keep arriving on time
use_when: Use when a front player misses a common threshold such as arm length but the tape and disruption profile argue for an exception
avoid_when: Do not use if the prototype miss is actually showing up as a real on-field limitation
sample_phrase: Prototype thresholds are lighter than ideal
notes: so the rush plan and play strength have to keep outweighing the length questions.

plain_english: Straight-line speed is present but transitions and route-mirror flexibility can still be stressed
mechanism: Acceleration shows up easily but the feet and hips do not always stay connected through route transitions
trigger: When he has to open redirect and drive from compromised leverage
nfl_consequence: Recovery speed becomes a cleanup tool instead of proactive coverage control
use_when: Use when an athletic defensive back still shows stiffness delayed transitions or angle-cleanup needs
avoid_when: Do not use for players whose movement skill is already a strength
sample_phrase: Straight-line speed is easy to see
notes: but the transitions still need to stay smoother so the movement skill matches the raw testing.

plain_english: Hands can run hot and cold even when the athletic mismatch value is obvious
mechanism: Catch timing and hand placement drift when the ball arrives outside the ideal strike zone or through contact
trigger: When contested finishes or off-frame adjustments are required
nfl_consequence: Easy receiving value comes off the board and the mismatch role narrows
use_when: Use when the player brings mismatch athleticism but catch consistency remains uneven
avoid_when: Do not use if drops or hands are not a real concern
sample_phrase: The athletic mismatch profile is obvious
notes: but the hands have to become more consistent so easy wins do not come off the board.
column17: Useful for TE and WR receiving variance

plain_english: Generates force through contact and can jolt blockers off balance
mechanism: Builds rushes through chest pressure and displaces blockers before counters come alive
nfl_consequence: Compresses the pocket and shortens the rep for quarterbacks who cannot climb
use_when: Use for front-seven defenders who consistently displace contact and compress rush space with power
avoid_when: Do not use for speed-only rushers who win almost entirely around the arc
sample_phrase: Wins with real knock-back in contact
notes: collapsing the blocker surface before counters come alive.
column17: Useful for Reese and Bain style power notes

plain_english: Athletic ceiling is obvious but passing consistency still lags behind the tools
mechanism: Throwing mechanics placement and timing still drift too often from rep to rep
trigger: When the first answer is covered and the rep has to be won from structure
nfl_consequence: The projection tilts toward splash creation instead of dependable down-to-down passing
use_when: Use for quarterbacks whose size-athletic profile is rare but whose ball placement anticipation or decisions remain uneven
avoid_when: Do not use for established polished passers
notes: but the passing game still needs more consistency and anticipation before the projection fully stabilizes.
column17: Useful for developmental athletic QB language

plain_english: The highlight catches are there but week-to-week consistency can swing
mechanism: Ball skills flash high-end but the hands timing and detail are not stable every week
trigger: When he has to finish through contact or work outside his frame repeatedly
nfl_consequence: The role becomes more volatile than the raw talent suggests
use_when: Use for receivers with clear ball-winning ability but uneven consistency polish or maturity
avoid_when: Do not use for stable volume earners with clean week-to-week profiles
sample_phrase: The catch-point talent shows up in flashes
notes: but the full profile still needs more snap-to-snap consistency.
